reasoning:
The most critical factor here is the Bank of England (BoE) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting schedule and anticipated actions. The next MPC meeting after March 30 is scheduled for May 8, 2025. This means for the first two resolution dates (April 6 and April 29), the Bank Rate will almost certainly remain at its current 4.50%. The daily SONIA rate will fluctuate as white noise tightly around the 4.455-4.457% range. Because the recent march-end values (around 4.4558) are slightly below the trailing two-week average, short-term regression to the mean implies a >50% chance of the rate resolving higher for April dates.

Moving further out to June 28 and September 26, the market consensus strongly expects the BoE to deliver rate cuts (with targets of 3.75% to 4.00% by the end of 2025) across the May, August, and November meetings. Once a single 25-bps cut occurs, the SONIA rate will drop structurally below 4.30%, meaning any noise will not bridge the gap back up to 4.456%. Thus, the probability of an increase drops sharply to account for the probability of rate cuts. 

By March 2026, the horizon is wide enough that inflation or shifting macro conditions could prompt the BoE to pause or theoretically resume hikes, meaning entropy increases again and forces our long-tail estimate back closer to 0.5, adhering strictly to the principle of scaling probability with predictive uncertainty in distant horizons.
